title: impatiens fissibractea
intro: Impatiens fissibractea, also known as the yellow balsam or poor man's balsam, is a flowering plant species native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of alkaloids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Impatiens fissibractea are said to include its potential to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate digestive problems. Herbal preparations of the plant are typically made by infusing its leaves or stems in hot water to produce a tea, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
